intro: C++ is a strongly typed, compiled systems language that emphasizes hardware optimization, resource control, and predictable deterministic memory lifecycles through RAII. JavaScript is a dynamically typed, interpreted web scripting engine optimized for event-driven interfaces, flexible object shapes, and non-blocking asynchronous interaction workflows. Developers migrate from C++ to JavaScript to accelerate user interface iterations, simplify full-stack web architectures, and abstract low-level memory issues.

diffs.from: Static, strict type matching enforcing structural layouts, template metaprogramming, and explicit inheritance.
diffs.to: Dynamic, loose type resolution relying on runtime evaluations, prototypes, and implicit type conversions.

diffs.from: Verbose syntax featuring header files, scoping operators (`::`), explicit pointer abstractions, and templates.
diffs.to: Flexible C-style syntax using dynamic variable definitions, inline closure declarations, and object literals.

diffs.from: Explicit manual or RAII-driven stack and heap tracking via smart pointers (`std::unique_ptr`).
diffs.to: Automatic memory allocation tracking handled implicitly by a tracing garbage collection subsystem.

diffs.from: Multi-threaded synchronization models requiring explicit locks (`std::mutex`), conditions, and atomics.
diffs.to: Single-threaded event loop engine driving asynchronous executions via `Promises` and `async/await` blocks.

diffs.from: High-performance systems algorithms, file streams, thread management, and complex template containers.
diffs.to: Web-centric abstractions tracking JSON parsing, regex pipelines, URI manipulation, and DOM/fetch interfaces.

examples.title: Asynchronous Web Fetching
examples.description: Replacing low-level manual network socket loops with an abstract promise chain.
examples.fromCode: // Extremely low-level conceptual C++ socket connection mockup\n#include <iostream>\n// Requires substantial system socket setup code or inclusion of heavy libraries like Boost.Asio

examples.title: Dynamic Map Configuration
examples.description: Replacing rigid compile-time map tracking configurations with fluid literal objects.

tips.title: Beware of Loose Equality Hazards
tips.body: Avoid using the double-equals operator (`==`) because JavaScript attempts runtime conversions. Always enforce strict verification using the triple-equals comparison (`===`).

tips.title: Understand Prototypal Inheritance Mechanics
tips.body: JavaScript does not utilize classic static class blueprints. Even modern `class` wrappers rely on runtime prototype modifications and linkages.

tips.title: Avoid Blocking the Main Event Loop
tips.body: JavaScript relies on a single thread. Do not implement heavy synchronous mathematical processing blocks that could freeze script rendering execution cycles.

tips.title: Compile Performance Blocks using WebAssembly
tips.body: If specific subroutines demand absolute C++ computation speed, compile those isolated assets down to WebAssembly targets to run efficiently inside JS runtimes.
